name: Polish Sausage (Kielbasa)
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces Beef shin, cut into half-inch dice, chilled
14 ounces Fresh pork fat, cut into half-inch dice, chilled
1 1 1/4 pounds Lean, trimmed pork, cut into dice
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together in a small bowl the salt, pepper, paprika, marjoram, savory, and garlic.
2. In a food processor, combine half the beef, half the pork fat, half the ice water, and half the mixed seasonings. Process until very fine.
3. Mix the remaining ingredients together in a bowl, then process the mixture half at a time to coarse grind. Mix all together in a bowl, cover, and refrigerate for 24 hours.
4. Stuff casings using a sausage stuffer or a horn attached to a meat grinder. Tie links 10 to 30 inch lengths depending on preference.
5. Hang sausages in a cool, airy place to dry until touch, or simply refrigerate uncovered for 24 hours. They can be held in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and frozen for longer storage.
6. Cook in the usual manner. Yield: about 2 3/4 pounds.
